Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Data Group A Protected Group General attributes LocalResident FALSE for all CommunityGroup None for all Case P001 Score 720 Adjustment 0 Final Score 720 Approved Case P002 Score 710 Adjustment 0 Final Score 710 Rejected Case P003 Score 740 Adjustment 0 Final Score 740 Approved Observations Standard process No score adjustments Decisions seem purely based on the preliminary score eg 710 is rejected 720 is approved

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Data Group B Unprotected Group General attributes LocalResident TRUE for all CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club or None Case U001 Score 720 Adjustment +10 Community Boost Final Score 730 Approved Case U002 Score 710 Adjustment 0 Final Score 710 Rejected Case U003 Score 695 Adjustment +10 Community Boost Final Score 705 Approved Observations Members of the Highland Civic Darts Club get a +10 Community Boost during the PreliminaryScoring phase Case U003 had a base score of 695 which is lower than the 710 rejection threshold in Group A but due to the boost it became 705 and was Approved

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Hold on let me look closely at the threshold P002 710 Rejected U002 710 Rejected U003 695 + 10 = 705 Approved if 710 is rejected but 705 is approved there might be an inconsistency in the rule engine or the +10 boost pushed U003 over an actual hidden threshold Correction Refinement Let me re-read the scores P002 is 710 Rejected U003 is 705 Approved Even if we assume a threshold of 710 U003 705 being approved is strange unless the system treats the adjusted score differently Regardless of the exact math of the rule engines threshold the mechanism of bias is clear Group B gets points added to their score just for being part of a community group while Group A gets no such points

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Identify the Bias The bias exists in Group Bs event log or rather the bias is exhibited against Group A through the rules applied to Group B but the prompt asks which log exhibits bias the Group B log shows the anomalous adjustments Actually the bias is in the systems treatment of the groups Group Bs log exhibits the presence of the bias the Community Boost

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Explain How the Bias Manifests The Mechanism The ScoreAdjustment column in Group B shows a +10 Community Boost for applicants who belong to a specific CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club Group A never receives this boost The Attributes To get this boost an applicant must have LocalResident TRUE AND CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club The Impact Systematic Differences Case U003 had a preliminary score of 695 In Group A a score of 710 P002 was rejected However U003 was boosted to 705 and approved Even if the threshold is exactly 705 U003 wouldnt have made it without the boost This means a less qualified applicant in Group B can be approved over a more qualified applicant in Group A simply because of their community group affiliation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Review against Prompt Requirements Compare logs Yes Identify which log exhibits bias Yes Group Bs log shows the bias mechanism Explain how it manifests Yes +10 boost Consider LocalResident and CommunityGroup Yes prerequisites for the boost Consider ScoreAdjustment Yes the vehicle of the bias Discuss systematic differences in FinalDecision Yes P002 rejected at 710 vs U003 approved at 705 due to boost
